source: Rocky Linux Admin Guide, ch. 12.5 The crontab file (pp. 215-216)

questions.q: A crontab line has six fields. What are the first five (in order) and the sixth?
options: minute, hour, day of month, month, day of week - then the command/script to run
hour, minute, month, day, year - then the command
command, minute, hour, day, month - then the day of week
second, minute, hour, day, month - then the command
questions.why: Fields 1-5 are minute, hour, day-of-month, month, day-of-week; field 6 is the command or script.

questions.q: What is the valid range for the MINUTE field (field 1)?
What is the valid range for the HOUR field (field 2)?
In the day-of-week field (field 5), which values represent Sunday?
options: 0 or 7 (both mean Sunday); the range is 0-7
questions.why: Day of week runs 0-7 where 0 and 7 both mean Sunday.

questions.q: In the line '10 4 1 * * /root/scripts/backup.sh', when does the task run?
options: At 04:10 on the 1st of every month
questions.why: minute 10, hour 4, day-of-month 1, any month, any weekday: 04:10 on the 1st of each month.

questions.q: What must the command field use for paths, and why?
options: Absolute paths (and ideally redirects), because the job runs unattended with no interactive terminal
questions.why: Scheduled tasks must use absolute paths (and preferably redirect their output), since they run without a terminal or shell context.
